status=open
reason=Host environment ENOSPC blocks all subprocess-based tools (Bash, Agent subagents, LSP for Go) for the full duration of this attempt. Every Bash invocation fails before running any command with `ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-862c21e7-20260708T210441-e1df1361'` — the sandbox cannot even create its working directory. This was verified repeatedly, including via a fresh subagent (isolated Agent call), which hit the identical error. Confirmed retryable, not code-related: `/tmp` on the host is full, unrelated to this bead's scope. Investigation performed with Read-only tools (which do not require the broken sandbox): - cli/internal/bead/migrator.go and cli/internal/bead/import_postgres.go already contain what appears to be a complete implementation of the source-loading and limit-handling behavior described in this bead: loadImportCorpusForAxon reads both `.ddx/beads.jsonl` and `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl` via readImportCorpus (which returns nil, nil for missing/empty files), dedupeImportedBeads dedupes by bead ID with active-corpus-first precedence, and importJSONLCorpusToAxon applies MigrateAxonOptions.Limit before calling prepareImportedAxonCorpus (the write-prep step). - cli/internal/bead/import_postgres_test.go already contains TestImporter_Apply_RespectsLimit, matching AC2 verbatim (asserts Limit=2 imports only the first two prepared beads/events). - I could not find TestImporter_Source_LoadsActiveAndArchiveJSONL or TestMigrateToAxonNoSources (AC1, AC3) in the files inspected so far, but could not run `grep`/`go test` to confirm their absence across the whole package, or confirm current green/red status of the suite, because Bash is unavailable. No code was written and no commit was made this attempt, since I cannot verify AC4/AC5/AC6 (go test, lefthook) without a working shell. Retry once the host frees /tmp disk space or this bead is redispatched to a healthy worker.
suggested_action=retry with a smart agent

no_changes
rationale: status: open
reason: Host environment ENOSPC blocks all subprocess-based tools (Bash, Agent subagents, LSP for Go) for the full duration of this attempt. Every Bash invocation fails before running any command with `ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-862c21e7-20260708T210441-e1df1361'` — the sandbox cannot even create its working directory. This was verified repeatedly, including via a fresh subagent (isolated Agent call), which hit the identical error. Confirmed retryable, not code-related: `/tmp` on the host is full, unrelated to this bead's scope.

Investigation performed with Read-only tools (which do not require the broken sandbox):
- cli/internal/bead/migrator.go and cli/internal/bead/import_postgres.go already contain what appears to be a complete implementation of the source-loading and limit-handling behavior described in this bead: loadImportCorpusForAxon reads both `.ddx/beads.jsonl` and `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl` via readImportCorpus (which returns nil, nil for missing/empty files), dedupeImportedBeads dedupes by bead ID with active-corpus-first precedence, and importJSONLCorpusToAxon applies MigrateAxonOptions.Limit before calling prepareImportedAxonCorpus (the write-prep step).
- cli/internal/bead/import_postgres_test.go already contains TestImporter_Apply_RespectsLimit, matching AC2 verbatim (asserts Limit=2 imports only the first two prepared beads/events).
- I could not find TestImporter_Source_LoadsActiveAndArchiveJSONL or TestMigrateToAxonNoSources (AC1, AC3) in the files inspected so far, but could not run `grep`/`go test` to confirm their absence across the whole package, or confirm current green/red status of the suite, because Bash is unavailable.

No code was written and no commit was made this attempt, since I cannot verify AC4/AC5/AC6 (go test, lefthook) without a working shell. Retry once the host frees /tmp disk space or this bead is redispatched to a healthy worker.
